Topic hub

Plant Problems

A troubleshooting hub for plant problems in urban gardens, hydroponic setups, and indoor herb systems, focused on diagnosis before quick fixes.

2 published guides Start here for the full plant problems reading path
Browse archive View all articles
Plant Problems March 9, 2026

Common Problems With Potted Herbs: How to Diagnose Yellow Leaves, Wilting, and Weak Growth

Most potted herb problems are not isolated mysteries. Yellow leaves, wilting, and weak growth usually trace back to light, drainage, containers, or routine drift.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Plant Problems March 5, 2026

Why Are Basil Leaves Turning Yellow Indoors?

Yellow basil rarely has a single universal cause. The pattern of yellowing, the growing medium, and the recent care routine usually tell the story.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide

Plant problems are easiest to misread when the symptom is dramatic. Yellow leaves, stretch, slow growth, and stalled herbs all feel urgent, so growers tend to jump directly to products or nutrient changes. In small-space systems, that usually creates more noise than clarity.

Diagnosis before intervention

The most useful troubleshooting pattern is consistent:

  1. describe the symptom precisely
  2. identify the most likely system causes
  3. apply the smallest fix that matches the evidence
  4. wait long enough to read the response

That sounds simple, but it is the opposite of how many gardening tips are presented online. Quick-fix advice often skips the diagnosis step entirely.

Why symptoms repeat across systems

Urban gardening, hydroponics, and indoor growing look different, but many symptoms come from the same underlying causes: poor light, stressed roots, unstable moisture, overcrowding, or temperature issues. A basil plant in soil and a lettuce plant in hydroponics can both underperform for reasons that trace back to root-zone stability and light management.

That is why this hub links troubleshooting content back to setup content. A diagnosis article should do more than name the problem. It should help you spot the setup choice or habit that keeps causing it.

What this hub helps with

Use the beginner section to learn how to think diagnostically. Use the setup section if you want to prevent common failures through better media, lighting, or environmental fit. Use the troubleshooting section when a crop is already declining. Use the advanced section when you need to translate symptoms into fixture, nutrient, or container decisions.

The point of this hub is to help you connect symptoms, likely causes, and the next fix worth trying.

How to use this hub

Move from overview to the next useful guide

Plant problems are usually symptoms of a mismatch somewhere in the system. This hub organizes diagnosis-led guides around symptoms, likely causes, quick fixes, and deeper explanations that connect back to light, roots, and environment.

Guide pathways

Explore the topic by stage and intent

These grouped sections keep the hub useful for beginners, active growers, and readers solving a specific problem.

Topic pathway

Start with diagnosis-led guides that explain how to read plant symptoms without jumping to conclusions.

Grow Light Distance for Seedlings: How Close Is Too Close?

Seedlings usually need grow lights much closer than beginners expect, but the exact distance depends on fixture strength, lens design, and plant response.

Topic pathway

Setup guides

Prevention usually starts with the right system choices, not with rescue products.

Soil & Fertilizers February 26, 2026

Potting Mix vs. Raised Bed Soil for Containers: What Actually Belongs in Pots

Container plants need air space, drainage, and predictable structure. That is why raised bed soil and in-ground blends usually disappoint in pots.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Grow Lights February 22, 2026

How Many Hours Should Grow Lights Run for Herbs?

A longer light schedule is not always a better one. Herbs need enough daily light, but they also benefit from a stable dark period and realistic fixture placement.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide

Troubleshooting

Use these when growth has already slowed, yellowed, or stretched and you need an orderly diagnosis path.
